_purpose: Canonical golden vectors for the claude-footprint methodology (CO2, cost, and water). Source of truth shared with downstream consumers (TokenClimate SaaS keeps a byte-identical copy in tests/fixtures/ and checks it weekly). Any change to data/factors.json or data/prices.json MUST update these expected values in the same commit.

_formulas.co2_grams: ((input_tokens + cache_creation_tokens) * factor_in + cache_read_tokens * (factor_in * cache_read_factor) + output_tokens * factor_out) / 1e6
_formulas.cost_usd: (input_tokens * price_in + cache_creation_tokens * (price_in * cache_write_multiplier) + cache_read_tokens * (price_in * cache_read_multiplier) + output_tokens * price_out) / 1e6
_formulas.water_liters: ((input_tokens + cache_creation_tokens) * water_in + cache_read_tokens * (water_in * cache_read_factor) + output_tokens * water_out) / 1e6 (same shape as co2_grams; equivalently co2_grams * 3.32/287)

_token_semantics: input_tokens here is the UNCACHED input (Anthropic usage.input_tokens). cache_creation_tokens (cache write) is separate. Note: the plugin DB column input_tokens stores input + cache_write; these vectors keep them split, as captured from the JSONL.

_constants._family_mapping: Models are mapped to a family by data/factors.json family_patterns (resolve_family in scripts/lib-factors.sh): claude tiers fable/opus/sonnet/haiku, plus provider-agnostic tiers frontier (Opus-equiv), mid (Sonnet-equiv), small (Haiku-equiv) and default (Sonnet-equiv fallback) for every other agent.

_rounding: All token counts are multiples of 10,000 so every expected value is EXACT: <= 3 decimals for co2_grams, <= 4 decimals for cost_usd, <= 5 decimals for water_liters. This makes the vectors implementation-agnostic: the bash plugin prints co2 at 4 decimals / cost + water at 6 decimals, the SaaS rounds to fewer, and both land on the exact value. Runners compare with a relative tolerance of 1e-6.
_excluded_semantics: Vectors with excluded=true use a model matching factors.json exclude_models (default: '<synthetic>', i.e. non-billed synthetic turns with no real inference). expected_* is null: the plugin stores co2_grams=0 / water_liters=0 with excluded=1 (raw tokens kept, no estimate). Note: unlike the Claude-only era, non-Anthropic models (gpt/gemini/glm/...) are NO LONGER excluded — they map to a provider-agnostic tier and DO get an estimate (see _family_mapping). expected_cost_usd is null for non-Claude families because the footprint pipeline takes their cost from tokscale rather than computing it.

vectors.description: Fable 5, full mix of all four token kinds
Opus, plain input/output, no cache traffic
Sonnet, cache_read dominant - shape of a real Claude Code session (dated model id resolved by family pattern)
Haiku, full mix of all four token kinds
Opus, cache_write only: full input factor for CO2, 1.25x input price
Sonnet, cache_read only: 0.08x input factor for CO2, 0.1x input price
Synthetic/non-billed turns (<synthetic>): no real inference, no estimate fabricated
Fable family via the mythos model id
Opus, large session with heavy cache traffic
Haiku, small symmetric session, no cache
Non-Anthropic frontier model (gpt-5) -> frontier tier (Opus-equivalent factors). Cost from tokscale, not computed here.
gpt-5-codex with heavy cache_read -> frontier tier; cache_read at 0.08x input factor
gpt-4o-mini -> small tier (Haiku-equivalent factors); 'mini' matched before frontier patterns
gpt-5-mini must resolve to small (not frontier): the 'mini' pattern precedes the gpt-5 pattern
Unknown real model not matched by any specific pattern -> default tier (Sonnet-equivalent fallback)
